conjecture: Closed constraint walks where you snap back to the lattice after each step stay bounded
decomposition_raw: [{"id": "S1", "statement": "For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, snap(snap(p)) = snap(p)", "verifier": "snap_idempotence", "args": {"N": 1000}, "reason": "Confirms snapping a lattice point leaves it unchanged, ensuring each walk step starts at a valid lattice point"}, {"id": "S2", "statement": "For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, the distance between p and snap(p) is at most 1/√3", "verifier": "covering_radius", "args": {"N": 1000}, "reason": "Ensures post-step points are within a fixed bounded distance of the hexagonal lattice, preventing arbitrarily large jumps between lattice points"}, {"id": "S3", "statement": "For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, snap(p) lands in exactly 12 sectors relative to the nearest lattice point", "verifier": "dodecet_cardinality", "args": {"N": 1000}, "reason": "Confirms the snap function is well-defined with no ambiguous nearest lattice points"}, {"id": "S4", "statement": "Any closed constraint walk of 100 steps where each step is followed by snapping to the hexagonal lattice stays within distance 5 of the origin", "verifier": "drift_bounded", "args": {"steps": 100, "bound": 5}, "reason": "Directly verifies the core claim that closed constraint walks do not drift unbounded from the origin"}]

sub_conjectures.statement: For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, snap(snap(p)) = snap(p)
sub_conjectures.reason: Confirms snapping a lattice point leaves it unchanged, ensuring each walk step starts at a valid lattice point

sub_conjectures.statement: For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, the distance between p and snap(p) is at most 1/√3
sub_conjectures.reason: Ensures post-step points are within a fixed bounded distance of the hexagonal lattice, preventing arbitrarily large jumps between lattice points

sub_conjectures.statement: For 1000 random points in the Euclidean plane, snap(p) lands in exactly 12 sectors relative to the nearest lattice point
sub_conjectures.reason: Confirms the snap function is well-defined with no ambiguous nearest lattice points

sub_conjectures.statement: Any closed constraint walk of 100 steps where each step is followed by snapping to the hexagonal lattice stays within distance 5 of the origin
sub_conjectures.reason: Directly verifies the core claim that closed constraint walks do not drift unbounded from the origin
